articles.title: Kimberly-Clark Corporation $KMB Shares Sold by California Public Employees Retirement System
articles.summary: The California Public Employees Retirement System reduced its holdings in Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) by 17.6% during the third quarter, selling 216,603 shares and owning 1,015,581 shares valued at $126.28 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their stakes, with 76.29% of the stock owned by hedge funds and institutions. Kimberly-Clark recently announced a quarterly dividend increase to $1.28 per share, while analysts have a mixed outlook on the stock, with an average target price of $116.42.

articles.title: ArrowMark Colorado Holdings LLC Acquires 105,600 Shares of Kenvue Inc. $KVUE
articles.summary: ArrowMark Colorado Holdings LLC significantly increased its stake in Kenvue Inc. by 132.1%, purchasing an additional 105,600 shares, bringing their total to 185,557 shares valued at approximately $3.01 million. Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE) reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations with $0.27 EPS and $3.78 billion in revenue, and pays a quarterly dividend of $0.2075 per share, annualizing to a roughly 4.7% yield. Despite a high payout ratio of 107.79% and an average analyst rating of "Hold" with a target price of $19.58, institutional ownership in the company remains high, with other major firms also adjusting their positions.

articles.title: Target opens 5 new stores, with No. 2000 located in North Carolina
articles.summary: Target has reached a retail milestone by opening its 2,000th store in Fuquay-Varina, North Carolina, along with four other new locations. This expansion is part of the company's plan to open over 30 stores and remodel more than 130 in 2026, backed by a $5 billion capital expenditure. Despite recent sales declines and job cuts, Target aims for net sales growth this year, believing its strong balance sheet will support these expansion efforts.

articles.title: Target Corporation (TGT) PT Lifted to $120 at JPMorgan After Q4 Results
articles.summary: JPMorgan has increased its price target on Target Corporation (TGT) to $120 from $115, maintaining a Neutral rating, following the company's Q4 results. Target also revealed a multi-year growth strategy, focusing on enhancing product categories, improving customer experience through digital and in-store upgrades, advancing technology, and investing over $1 billion in operations and $5 billion in capital for supply chain, technology, and store expansions in 2026. The plan aims to provide a consistent and practical experience for value-conscious customers.

articles.title: Kroger Leadership Pivot: $90 Target Price Backed by New CEO & AI
articles.summary: Kroger (KR) delivered an encouraging fourth-quarter report, showcasing positive market share gains and announcing Greg Foran as the new CEO. Foran, formerly of Walmart U.S., plans a "back-to-basics" approach focusing on fresh food, well-run stores, and cost management, while the company also invests heavily in AI for supply chain optimization and new customer-facing shopping features. TIKR identifies Kroger as a stable cash flow generator with a target price of $89, indicating a 20% potential return, based on its digital acceleration, profitable media network, and strategic expansion.

articles.title: DoubleLine ETF Adviser LP Purchases New Holdings in Kimberly-Clark Corporation $KMB
articles.summary: DoubleLine ETF Adviser LP has acquired a new position in Kimberly-Clark Corporation, purchasing 17,622 shares valued at approximately $2.19 million in the third quarter. Kimberly-Clark has also increased its quarterly dividend to $1.28 per share, representing a 5.2% yield, with the ex-dividend date on March 6th. Despite a slight dip in revenue year-over-year, the company reported Q4 EPS of $1.86, beating analyst estimates, and analysts maintain a consensus "Hold" rating with a target price of $116.42.

articles.title: Boothbay Fund Management LLC Purchases 28,020 Shares of Urban Outfitters, Inc. $URBN
articles.summary: Boothbay Fund Management LLC increased its stake in Urban Outfitters by 26.9%, purchasing an additional 28,020 shares to reach a total of 132,224 shares valued at approximately $9.45 million. This comes as Urban Outfitters exceeded earnings expectations with an EPS of $1.43 and revenue of $1.80 billion, though analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with a target price of $86.25. Insider selling, including by CEO Richard Hayne, has been noted recently, but insiders retain a significant ownership of 31.6% of the stock.

articles.title: Netflix, Target, Transocean And A Financial Stock On CNBC's 'Final Trades'
articles.summary: CNBC's "Halftime Report Final Trades" featured analysts recommending Netflix as a defensive stock with a $115 price target, Target due to recent price reductions on over 3,000 items, and Transocean following its acquisition of Valaris, with a raised price target of $7.5. Additionally, CME Group Inc. was recommended as a financial stock, having recently reported stronger-than-expected Q2 earnings. The article also provides the latest price action for each company discussed.

articles.title: Banque Transatlantique SA Acquires 17,438 Shares of Walmart Inc. $WMT
articles.summary: Banque Transatlantique SA increased its stake in Walmart Inc. by 1.4% in the third quarter, acquiring an additional 17,438 shares and bringing its total holdings to 1,285,863 shares valued at $132.37 million. This makes Walmart its ninth-largest holding, representing 3.2% of the fund's portfolio. articles.title: Target (NYSE: TGT) executive awarded RSUs and performance units; shares withheld for tax
articles.summary: Target executive Pratabkumar Vemana received 20,949 restricted stock units and 7,291 performance-based restricted stock units on March 11, 2026, as part of the company's 2020 Long-Term Incentive Plan. Concurrently, 625 common shares were withheld at $119.88 per share to cover tax obligations from a prior performance-based award. Following these transactions, Vemana directly holds 48,251 Target common shares.

articles.title: How Target-Heavy Balanced Funds Could Magnify Systemic Risk Amid Retail Sector Volatility
articles.summary: Balanced funds with significant holdings in Target Corp. could increase systemic risk if the retail sector experiences sharp downturns. This is because correlated drawdowns might force broad portfolio rebalancing by institutional investors, potentially depressing Target's share price beyond its fundamentals. Such movements could be triggered by rising volatility, as observed between 2022-2024.

articles.title: Target Corp Stock (ISIN: US8725901040) Bets $2 Billion on 2026 Turnaround Amid Recovery Signals
articles.summary: Target Corporation is investing $2 billion in 2026 for store upgrades, staffing, and merchandising enhancements to drive a turnaround, following strong Q4 2025 earnings and the first positive comparable sales signal in months. The investment aims to bridge the perception gap with value leaders like Walmart and Costco, capitalizing on resilient consumer spending and a dividend yield near 5%. The stock has rebounded sharply, reflecting investor confidence in its recovery strategy.

articles.title: Target Corp Stock (ISIN: US8725901040) Recovers on Strong Q4 Beat and $2B Turnaround Plan
articles.summary: Target Corp (ISIN: US8725901040) has seen its stock surge 36% since November lows, reaching around $115, following a strong Q4 EPS beat and a positive FY2026 guidance. The company plans a $2 billion investment for store remodels and operational enhancements, aiming to drive long-term growth. Despite a slight revenue miss, cost controls and operational efficiencies led to an adjusted EPS of $2.44, surpassing estimates and reigniting investor interest amidst a challenging retail environment.

articles.title: Target's new CEO cuts prices to win back shoppers, boost revenue
articles.summary: Target's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, is implementing a strategy of significant price cuts on over 3,000 products, following a playbook used by his predecessor. This move aims to boost demand and reverse three years of declining sales, alongside a $6 billion investment in capital expenditures and operating expenses to improve stores, accelerate deliveries, and enhance technology. While analysts see this as a step in the right direction, they emphasize that price cuts alone may not be enough to regain market share from competitors like Walmart and Amazon, and sustained investor patience will be crucial.
